ShortDescription.Value: Adult trees spread naturally, spawning saplings nearby that grow to maturity over time.

Publish.LongDescription:
Natural Regrowth lets your forests grow on their own.
Mature trees periodically spawn saplings on nearby open ground. Each sapling then grows to maturity through the game's normal tree growth simulation, and once grown, begins spreading in turn. Over time, wooded areas fill in and expand the way real forests do, without any manual painting.
[h3]Features[/h3] [list] [*] Adult trees spawn saplings on suitable nearby terrain [*] Saplings grow naturally using the base game's growth system [*] A local density cap prevents runaway overgrowth and keeps performance healthy [*] Adjustable reproduction rate, from a slow trickle to rapid spread [/list]
[h3]Settings[/h3] [list] [*] [b]Reproduction Rate[/b] - how quickly trees spread. Set to 0% to pause reproduction entirely. [*] [b]Local Density Cap[/b] - the maximum number of trees allowed in a small area before spreading stops there. Lower values keep forests sparser; higher values allow denser growth. [/list]
[h3]Performance[/h3] The mod is built on the game's job system (DOTS/ECS) and uses scheduled per-tree timers rather than per-frame checks, so it scales to large, heavily forested maps without meaningful overhead.
[h3]Notes[/h3] This initial release covers trees. Bush and shrub reproduction may come in a future update.
Publish.ChangeLog: Initial release. Adult trees spread saplings naturally, with adjustable rate and a local density cap.
